name: SMART-1
fact: ESA's first lunar mission. Used solar-electric (ion) propulsion to spiral out from a geostationary transfer orbit to lunar capture — a 14-month, fuel-efficient transit that pioneered low-thrust trajectories for ESA's later interplanetary missions. Deliberately impacted the lunar surface in 2006 for an observed end-of-life experiment.
capability: First operational use of ion propulsion for a lunar mission — a template for ESA's later BepiColombo and Hera deep-space missions.
